Do you speak Japanese?

The  name of a national language is often the same as the national adjective.

American literature (     american literature) All words of this kind (including adjectives) begin with capital letters.

However, there are a number of exceptions. Some of these are listed in 321.3.

meanings (e.g. the Japanese; the Swiss). >321.2  below for more examples.

the Mexicans); words ending in -ese, and Swiss, remain unchanged for plural The  plural expression is usually the same as the adjective + -s (e.g. the Greeks, Usually the singular noun is the same as the adjective (e.g. Greek, Mexican).
